description_long: This helicopter features a futuristic design with a large, circular blue ring structure forming its lower section, possibly serving as a stabilizing or propulsion element. The main body is constructed from a gray, metallic-looking material with a blocky, angular shape and a central fuselage extending forward. The rear of the helicopter displays three prominent red exhaust or thruster ports, suggesting a hybrid or advanced propulsion system. The upper section includes a flat, rectangular area that may house avionics or serve as a landing platform, while the lower ring is segmented and reinforced with crossbars. The surface texture is predominantly matte with a tiled pattern, and the blue ring contrasts sharply with the gray body. No traditional rotor blades, tail rotor, or cockpit windows are visible, indicating a highly unconventional or conceptual aircraft.
description: A futuristic helicopter with a large blue circular ring base, blocky gray body, and three red rear thrusters. The design is angular and lacks visible rotors or cockpit windows.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide blue ring, central gray body, and a flat, rectangular upper section.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, angular body, and the depth of the blue ring structure.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals three large red thruster ports and the segmented structure of the blue ring.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the pointed nose, angular body, and the crossbars within the blue ring.
attribute_landing_skids: not visible; the helicopter appears to rest on the circular ring structure instead of traditional skids.
